runs.root_cause: Progressive peak-date sellout on TGID 10118 (Keukenhof Entry Tickets) confirmed by three converging signals: availability proxy (count_days_available_30d declining 31→15), lead-time distribution collapse (30d+ bookings: 751 pre → 77 post, −90%), and experience-level S2C drop concentrated on TGID 10118.

Peak-season pricing increases (+3–8%) on key Vatican experiences drove LP2S decline, compounded by spring break traffic surge exceeding fixed timed-entry supply, reducing available booking dates and depressing S2C.

Progressive near-term inventory depletion through April spring break/Easter season drove S2C from 32.7% to 26.3%. S2C was 99.2% of Shapley; availability proxy and lead-time bucket query confirmed supply as mechanism.

Post-spring-break demand-quality collapse drove S2C from 32.7% to 26.3% across all Kualoa Ranch experiences. Secondary: All-Inclusive Package (37863) delisted from ~Apr 15, eliminating a second revenue stream for the final 14 days of the post period.

Vatican Museums CVR fell −0.63pp (4.47%→3.84%) because peak spring demand (+13% traffic) exceeded the Vatican's fixed timed-entry slot supply — shrinking available booking dates by 5.1 per 30-day window — while a secondary checkout failure on iOS (C2A) and Android (A2O) added a compounding −40% Shapley contribution.

S2C fell from 31.8% to 25.2% (−6.65pp, 70% of ΔCVR) because the post period captured the wind-down of Hawaii's spring break season: high-intent spring break visitors were replaced by lower-intent off-peak tourists. No supply, pricing, or UX change — purely a demand quality decline.
